shape_decode: cmp,vp,vpc,naive = _,L,W,_ : value_profile_cmplog RESOLVES, value_profile BLOCKS, cmplog+naive non-decisive -> i2s-pro (vpc's I2S lands the exact multibyte literal; vp's Hamming-prefix gradient stalls before it).

hypotheses.label: vpc's I2S enriches the exact multibyte literal in its corpus; value_profile's gradient does not

arm_repointing.note: Repoint operand_enrichment's enrichment baseline from its default cmplog-vs-naive to vpc(I2S-winner)-vs-value_profile(VP-blind-loser). Param repointing of the SAME byte_freq_ratio measurement at the W/L gate offsets, not a new tool.

target_literals.libxml2_6193: xmlns\0 (6-byte contiguous_literal at the SAX2.c:1170-1171 equality chain)

hypotheses.prediction: G1: i2s-pro predicts the exact multibyte target literal is ENRICHED in the vpc-resolving corpus at the gate offset and signed_target_enrich > 0, while value_profile (gradient-blind to the exact multibyte sequence) does NOT enrich it. Discriminated ALTERNATIVE: if value_profile's Hamming gradient could climb to the literal, the literal would appear in the VP corpus too and signed_target_enrich would be ~0 or negative.
hypotheses.status_note: KEEP / carry-forward per superset rule. This is the prior hypothesis verbatim (rule unchanged, no relaxed threshold). At round-0 scoring on the libxml2 corpus it returned rule_not_met for 6193 (the fixed-offset byte_freq_ratio model is mismatched: the gate compares a derived name[] buffer from xmlSplitQName, not a raw byte at a stable input offset, so the literal does not enrich at one gate offset). It is retained UNCHANGED so any other server / future libxml2 corpus re-scores it, and tried FIRST in decision_order, but the new value-distance hypothesis below is the primary discriminator for this shape.

hypotheses.label: vpc's I2S lands AT the exact multibyte literal; value_profile's Hamming gradient stalls a measurable distance short of it

arm_repointing.note: The shape's decisive pair is vpc(W) > vp(L). value_distance_reached natively computes per-arm min sliding-window Hamming distance to operand_literal for all four arms; the discriminating comparison here is vpc_min_distance (lands at literal) vs vp_min_distance (gradient stalls). winner_closer is evaluated on vpc-vs-vp, not vp-vs-cmp.

hypotheses.prediction: G1 (genuinely DIFFERENT mechanism than the refuted byte_freq_ratio enrichment): the i2s-pro / gradient-blind story predicts vpc's seeds reach the exact 6-byte 'xmlns\0' literal (vpc_min_distance ~ 0) while value_profile's prefix-Hamming gradient stalls a measurable distance short of the NUL-terminated 6-way equality chain (vp_min_distance substantially > 0), so winner_closer=true and distance_gap is large. The DISCRIMINATED ALTERNATIVE the refuted enrichment test could not rule out: that value_profile's CMP_MAP gradient actually DOES climb to within a byte of the literal (so vpc has no real distance advantage) -- if so vp_min_distance would be ~0, distance_gap ~ 0, winner_closer=false, REFUTING gradient-blind. Unlike byte_freq_ratio this uses a SLIDING WINDOW (no fixed-offset assumption), so it survives the derived-name[] / xmlSplitQName-output offset problem that made the enrichment test mismatched for this gate.
hypotheses.rule: winner_closer == true AND distance_gap >= 0.2 AND vpc_min_distance <= 0.05
hypotheses.caveat: operand width 6 is above the width<=2 chance-alignment flag, so this is not chance-prone. value_distance_reached reads on-disk seeds; libxml2 has no on-disk corpus on s4 -- the OWNING server (sB holds libxml2) scores this. Specified for ALL servers per cross-server rule; not marked unscorable for a corpus merely absent on the running server.

data_realities_respected: On-disk corpora exist ONLY for curl/harfbuzz/openthread/sqlite3 on s4; libxml2 lives on server B. Both feasible measurements (operand_enrichment, value_distance_reached) read seeds/corpus, so they are scored by the OWNING server (sB), NOT marked decidable:false here for a corpus absent on the running server (cross-server rule 4).

operand_enrichment skips structural/length gates and needs a fixed-offset VALUE gate with a contiguous target literal at a STABLE offset. libxml2_6193's gate compares a derived name[] buffer (xmlSplitQName output), so the literal is not at a fixed input offset -- this is why the fixed-offset enrichment rule did not hold and why value_distance_reached (sliding window, no fixed-offset assumption) is the better-matched second test.
value_distance_reached needs operand_literal + operand_width from the signature; libxml2_6193 supplies 'xmlns\0' / width 6 (above the width<=2 chance-alignment flag).

I2S is NOT lineage-tagged and value_profile is a feedback not a mutator: neither hypothesis uses seed_lineage:event_cooccurrence on an 'I2S event' or a 'gradient event'. Both measure corpus/seed CONTENT (byte enrichment, distance-to-operand), which is the only feasible window onto these effects.

joint/structural gates have different-sized W vs L seeds so byte-alignment fails -- relevant to libxml2_9435 (state_or_grammar / dispersed_multibyte structural namespace-assembly gate), which is excluded from both byte-aligned tests.

unscorable_seed_starved.note: These two are NOT decidable:false (no honest mechanism verdict). They are seed_starved: libxml2_8138 has W=1 L=7 and libxml2_9435 has W=2 L=30 in db/blockers.sqlite, below operand_enrichment / value_distance_reached's >=3-W,>=3-L floor. The deficit is a DATA-COLLECTION gap, not a non-discriminable mechanism -- route both to rank-10 re-bisection (tools/seed_bisect.py) to recover >=3 W seeds, then re-score. Until then they ride fallback:inconclusive as pending_data, distinct from a permanent decidable:false. Their mechanisms are plausibly discriminable once seeds exist: 8138 is a single_byte high-bit/IS_CHAR decode gate (operand_literal=null -> needs value_distance_reached on the decoded lead-byte sequence, or remains decidable:false if no stable operand emerges); 9435 is a structural namespace-assembly gate better matched to joint_necessity (struct_size_and_token_count) than to either byte-aligned tool.

notes.priors_kept: KEPT the single prior hypothesis i2s_multibyte_literal_splice_vp_gradient_blind verbatim (same rule, no relaxed threshold) per the superset/monotonic rule, tried FIRST in decision_order so the confirmed-elsewhere case re-validates.
notes.new_mechanism: ADDED one new hypothesis for the rule_not_met branch libxml2_6193: i2s_lands_literal_vp_gradient_stalls_distance. It is a GENUINELY DIFFERENT mechanism+measurement than the refuted one -- value-DISTANCE-to-operand via sliding window (registry tool value_distance_reached), not fixed-offset byte-frequency enrichment. It directly addresses WHY the enrichment test failed (the gate compares a derived name[] buffer, so there is no stable gate offset to enrich; sliding-window distance has no fixed-offset assumption) and discriminates the alternative the enrichment test could not (whether VP's gradient actually reaches the literal).
notes.decidable_false_subgroup: None marked permanently decidable:false in this round. The two unscorable branches (8138, 9435) are seed_starved -> rebisect_then_rescore (pending_data), per cross-server rule: a data-collection gap is not a non-discriminable mechanism. If re-bisection cannot recover >=3 W seeds for 8138 (single_byte, operand_literal=null -- no stable operand for any byte-aligned tool), it then legitimately becomes decidable:false; 9435 would route to joint_necessity. Recorded honestly rather than forced into a label.
notes.g1_novelty: The new hypothesis is falsifiable (winner_closer could come out false) and discriminating (vp_min_distance ~ 0 would refute gradient-blind). It is not a relaxed-threshold re-run of the refuted enrichment rule -- it is a different metric family (Hamming distance to operand vs corpus byte frequency at a fixed offset).
notes.g2_independence: Both measurements read corpus/seed CONTENT (byte enrichment / distance-to-operand-literal), independent of the resolve pattern (whether vpc reached the gate's resolving side) that DEFINED the shape -- no verifying-vpc-resolves-with-vpc-resolves.
notes.collapse_rationale: All 3 branches share the I2S-exact-sequence-vs-VP-gradient-blind story but they do NOT collapse into one scorable test: only 6193 has a fixed contiguous operand_literal ('xmlns\0') that either byte-aligned tool can target. 8138 (single_byte, null operand) and 9435 (structural, dispersed_multibyte) lack a stable operand and are additionally seed_starved, so they cannot share 6193's measurement and are routed separately.
